rationale: The citing paper explicitly manipulates experienced decision effort via perceptual fluency (Studies 2a–2c) and builds an effort-compatibility mechanism, which closely matches the cited paper’s core construct of preference fluency (subjective ease/difficulty) and attribution effects on choice. With this strong construct/method overlap plus a confirmed DOI citation (10.1509/jmkr.44.3.347), the reference is plausibly substantive rather than incidental.
